description_long: This is a traditional pitchfork featuring a long, straight wooden handle and three sharply curved metal tines. The tines are robust and slightly rusted, indicating frequent use and exposure to the elements. The handle is cylindrical, showing a natural wood grain with some discoloration and wear, especially near the base and where it meets the metal head. The tines are attached to the handle with a metal bracket, forming a triangular support structure for added strength. The overall appearance suggests it is a utilitarian tool, likely used for moving hay, leaves, or debris in urban gardens, parks, or maintenance areas. The visible patina and surface imperfections give it an authentic, well-used character.
description: A weathered pitchfork with a long wooden handle and three rusted metal tines, showing signs of frequent use and age.
description_view_0: The pitchfork is seen at an angle, clearly displaying the full length of the handle and the spread of the three tines.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the curvature and sharpness of the tines, with the handle appearing foreshortened.
description_view_2: A mirrored angle of view 0, showing the opposite side of the handle and tines, with similar details visible.
description_view_3: The pitchfork is viewed from the handle end, highlighting the alignment and spacing of the three tines as they project outward.
